column4: Evaluate the identity service as an identity provider to other Altir Apps
column6:
User Story
As a developer, I want to understand the existing capability of the identity service and evaluate it as a managed service, so that its functionalities can be used in the multi-tenant model. The clients applications and altir Apps must be using the managed identity service for authentication & authorization. 

Terminology - 
Clients are customers of Altir, ex., Soundful, Hyperfuel, Chip1, etc.
Apps are multi-tenancy model services developed and managed by Altir. The client application is using Apps as required. Ex., notifications, events, connect, tenant management, etc.
Acceptance criteria
Given: Currently, the identity service is a standalone service in all projects hence it has to be deployed separately which incurs extra resources.
When: It would be very beneficial if the identity service could be revamped to a managed service (Altir App).
Then: The identity service can also be developed as Altir App . The client  applications and other Altir Apps can use the identity service for auth & authz.
Architecture Guidance
To upgrade the identity service as a managed service (Altir App), the following functionalities should be evaluated in the multi-tenancy model -
Authentication -
Mode of authentications - 
 Client applications -
User authentication using password, OTP, third-party
Altir Apps - 
system to system authentication using API key, client credentials
service to service - 
client credential auth flow for service to service communication
Token verification by the microservice using the spring security mechanism (zero trust security model)
Formation of JWT token with custom claims
lambda-authorizer to verify the JWT token signature using the public key
Custom tables in the Keycloak schema
Custom endpoints for different clients
Authorization -
Configuration of the authorization model(roles & permissions) for clients & Altir Apps 
Need of custom tables in the Keycloak schema
Performance -  
Identify the possible performance bottleneck under 
high-volume requests
high-volume data
Infra - 
Multiple AWS accounts(clients & Altir Apps ) are involved in the call flow.
API gateway configurations in various AWS accounts clients & Altir Apps 
interaction among numerous AWS accounts.

 
API Guidance
NA
Design Guidance
NA

column4: Form validation - Setup common validation messages
column6:
Standardize validation messages to be used in most of the forms.

Standard validation messages:
{
  "required": "{{label}} is required",
  "phone": "Invalid phone",
  "string": {
    "email": "Invalid email",
    "min": "{{label}} must be at least {{value, number}} characters",
    "max": "{{label}} cannot exceed {{value, number}} characters",
    "length": "{{label}} must be exactly {{value, number}} characters"
  },
  "number": {
    "min": "The value must be greater than or equal to {{value, number}}",
    "max": "The value must be less than or equal to {{value, number}}",
    "moreThan": "The value must be greater than {{value, number}}",
    "lessThan": "The value must be less than {{value, number}}",
    "positive": "{{label}} must be a positive number",
    "negative": "{{label}} must be a negative number",
    "integer": "{{label}} must be an integer"
  },
  "date": {
    "minToday": "{{label}} cannot be in the past"
  }
},
Variables:
{{label}}  - field name (for example, "Project Name")
{{value, number}}  - formatted value as a number, different for every case. It can be min or max limit. For example, 50  or 4,000 
Standard values:
Quantity max number is 9,999,999,999 - 10 characters
Quantity is a positive integer number
Name max length: 50 
Text max length: 4,000

I'd like to address 3 concerns related to modals:
Close-on-outside-click. Modals with forms closing by outside click may be painful for users if some data has been entered/files uploaded. We need either prevent outside click for such modals or ask user directly if they really want to leave the modal and loose all entered data. In the latter case we might add such ask when user clicks Close or Cancel (if form was touched ).
Modal has prop close  which should be called like close('modal.name'). Shall we pass bound "close" method which would close itself instead? That would avoid the need to specify modal name inside of the modal and would help to prevent mistakes when part of code is copy/pasted
Currently ModalContext directly depends on all of the modals. That means that InnerLayout  loads ModalsProvider  which loads all the available modals code. And only then page can be rendered. Solution: lazy-load the MODALS  from config or convert modals into lazy components to load them only on demand.
`

column6:
Use partPrice.buyNowUrl  as a link on Order button. Please don't forget to have these props on a link component for external links: target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" 
Lead time - if it's not available on top level, try to use partPrice.leadTimeFormat  and partPrice.leadTimeWeeks (if none of them is equal to "Unknown" )
 
Packaging
packaging  comes in form of packaging: { key: 'packaging.25' } 
This is correct form for lookup, but usually we would get the full object. Here we have only unique key  property
This is how lookups come from backend
 
Keys are then processed in the next way: key = ${key}.${index} That means that packaging.25 would have value "N/A" .
This is how packaging  lookups would look once we get them from useLookups() :
 
Finally, what we need to do: if there's no packaging.value then we need to take all the packaging lookups and find corresponding lookup by key  and then display the lookup value in the column on UI

Summary
The Sourcing tab provides detailed pricing and vendor information for a selected part. It functions similarly to the side pane from the Project ➝ Pricing tab. Users can filter and view available vendor-specific data, including lead times, quantities, and packaging, while enabling a seamless ordering experience.

Filters:

Vendor: Filters by available vendors listed for the part.
Lead Time: Lists unique lead times as provided (e.g., "10 weeks").
Quantity: Filters by available quantities.
Packaging: Filters by packaging options (e.g., "Tape," "Reel").
Filters should be implemented on the UI side and should be based on the tab's displayed data. We don't need to display all the manufacturer names for example nor is it required to support a range for quantities/lead time for now since Lead time is just a string.
   - But, let's keep this on our backlog. We eventually need to move this to BE and support filtering <=/range of values.
CTA Behaviour:

column4: Improvements to MC1 Invitation and OTP Email Templates
column6:
Title: Revise MC1 Invitation and OTP Email Content for Better Readability and Usability
 Description:
The current email templates for MC1 Invitation and OTP email have a few grammatical errors and usability issues that can be improved for better user experience and clarity. These improvements aim to correct grammatical mistakes, enhance visual cues, and ensure consistency across all email communications.
 Acceptance Criteria:
Invitation Email Improvements:
Correct the two grammatical errors identified in Grammarly:
"state of the art" should be updated to "state-of-the-art."
Rearrange text to make the sentence more fluent: "seamlessly communicate with your Chip 1 representative" should be changed to "communicate with your Chip 1 representative seamlessly."
Update "MyChip1" to be bold (MyChip1) for better visibility and emphasis.


 

OTP Email Improvements:
Make the OTP stand out by:
Displaying the OTP in bold text.
Optionally, enclosing the OTP in a highlighted frame or distinct section for quicker scanning.
MyChip1 has spaces in OTP email but not in invitation email. Ensure consistent formatting of MyChip1 (e.g., spaces) with the OTP email for a uniform brand experience.

Without OTP emphasis:
 

With OTP emphasised:

 
Consistency and Brand Guidelines:
Ensure consistent use of bold text, spaces, and layout between all emails (Invitation and OTP emails).
Adhere to accessibility and readability standards, such as larger font sizes for critical information.
 Summary:
Correct grammatical errors and improve sentence fluency in the invitation email.
Apply bold formatting to "MyChip1" in the invitation email.
Update OTP email to display the OTP in bold and highlight it visually.
Ensure consistent formatting and spacing across all emails for MyChip1.
Perform a final QA review of updated emails.

Architecture Guidance
The following flags should be introduced in the entities, i.e., RFQ, quote, SalesOrder, Invoice  - 
isMigrated - The flag is set to true for migrated data, otherwise false.
isMc1Visible  - It is set to false for migrated data. otherwise true.
Note: Flags must be saved in both MongoDB & ES.
Entity filter criteria - The entities must be shown up on the UI with the following criteria 
isMigrated = false
isMigrated = true && isMc1Visible = true
Flag reset endpoint - An endpoint is required to reset the isMc1Visible flag after verifying the migrated data.
An internal endpoint /internat/resetMc1VsibleFlag shall be created in the transaction service to resent the flag.
It should support the bulk entity flag reset
It must allow all types of entities

Description:
To do the automation for Sign-up with mobile number, we need to connect to the db to fetch the OTP. Its all working fine in local, but seeing issues while running it from chip1 Jenkins.
It will have to connect to the following host to fetch the OTP
altir-non-prod-foundation.cwjbpih4sdlb.us-west-2.rds.amazonaws.com


Please help us to fix this
column9: 2 Jobs have been created. One in Altir's Jenkins  https://jenkins.altir.net/job/remote-fetch-otp/ . Another in Chip1 Jenkins  https://jenkins.chip1.info/job/remote-fetch-otp/  which can be used right away or as an example for your pipeline/script.   The way it works is the following:   your script tries to login into mc1 and OTP is generated/sent by keycloak   remote job in Altir's Jenkins is triggered, environment and users email are passed as parameters   this remote job queries Altir's Loki  {environment="$fnenv", app="keycloak"} |~ "Successfully sent OTP.*$users_email.*  and returns something like this:   Successfully sent OTP '301023' for 'c4f9e5ec-ccb0-42c4-8e5e-af0dab2a2e26' to 'sus@altir.co'    You can parse this output to get the OTP

column4: Identify and implement events-rerouting without downtime for events-client implementation
column6:
Approach:
To ensure a seamless transition from Spring Kafka to Events-Client without downtime or scheduled maintenance, a temporary queue has been introduced. This queue will be consumed asynchronously using Spring Kafka, and events will be republished to the new topics via the Events-Library. This approach guarantees a lossless migration of events while maintaining system reliability.

Services to be implemented:
fn-connect
fn-notification-publisher
fn-notification-dispatcher
chip1-transaction
chip1-part

Implement the following:
Integrate events-client library in chip1-transaction service to use multi-events client library.
Integrate events-client library in chip1-part service to use multi-events client library.
Integrate events-client library in fn-connect service to use multi-events client library.

column4: Project -> Part details - Investigate virtualization technics for table (500+ rows)
column6:
Current state: On Project → Parts details tab we render all the lines available on the project. There's no pagination on backend side, so all the lines are rendered at once. That means if a project has 500+ rows, it takes couple of seconds to render the table first time. If there are 5000 rows, depending on the data, browser can take more than 10-15 seconds to render and can show "Page is not responding" dialog. Even if page is rendered, it's hardly usable due to performance issues.

Virtualization technics allow to render actually 30 rows in a large scrollable container and change the positions of these rows by scrolling and inserting the correct values. That allows to have any number (virtual) rows rendered without perf. implications.

Things to note:
Find in page (ctrl+f or cmd+f) will not find the parts needed (most likely) because only 30-40 rows are rendered at a time instead of all on the page
When scrolling very fast, blank space may appear in the table until scrolling speed is slow enough (only then the needed rows would be rendered on the page)
As column width was dependent on the content displayed in all of the cells in a column, now we need to have fixed column widths to avoid cell widths jumping while scrolling (because different data is being inserted in the same 30-40 cells of a column)

column4: Change in the region id is not appended with existing region id
column6:
Summary:
Whenever user changing the region of the existing customer/supplier account by disapproving the previous region, in db currently both the records are displaying.
Steps to Reproduce:
1.Login into xCRM and Create an account a region.
2.Add customer account in one region(like Hongkong)
3.Add supplier account in another region(like US)
4.Change the region of the customer/supplier account by disapproving the previous region.
5.Check in the non-prod altir db, both the records are captured but not appended with the existing region id.
Expected Result:
when user changed the region in the customer/supplier account,then it should be appended with the updated region id.
Actual Result:
when user changed the region in the customer/supplier account,then both the records are displaying.
Additional Information:

column6:
Summary
There are 4 event types present in the MC1 and the new CRM apps.
notifications
connect
timeline
databits
As per the new event generation capability, a single event payload could carry all events and be processed separately in the events service.
The event service should have rules configured to handle the common event payload.
At first, the common event payload shall go to a common topic in the events service. Then the following rules shall be applied. If the common payload contains - 
notification event - The rule shall modify the payload and redirect to a topic from where the notification service shall consume it.
connect event - The rule shall modify the payload and redirect to a topic from where the connect service shall consume it.
timeline payload - The payload will be saved to the timeline database. cc   
databits payload - The rule shall modify the payload and redirect to a topic from where the databits service shall consume it. cc   

 
This mechanism will not be used by the existing notification and connect events now, but it will be used by the timeline and databits events.
Tech details
The generic event payload has 4 fields (each field can be nullable):
EventDto {
  ...
  payload: GenericEventPayload{
    notification: NotificationEventPayload{...},
    timeline: TimelineEventPayload{...},
    connect: ConnectEventPayload{...},
    dataBits: DataBitsPayload{...}
  }
}
Downstream services pass it to topic generic-events-inbound .
The scope of this ticket is event service rules for each generic event payload field: if it is not null, payload should be unwrapped and sent to the corresponding topic.
E.g. if field notification  is not null, payload should be transformed to:
EventDto {
  ...
  payload: NotificationEventPayload{...},
}
and sent to topic notifications-input 
Full list of target topics:


Payload field
Topic name
notification
notifications-input 
connect
mychip1--connect-outbound
timeline
timeline-inbound 
dataBits
data-bits-inbound

column4: QE: Validate Change account stage to inactive via scheduler job
column6:
Summary
As a developer, I would like to develop a capability to change the account stage to inactive If a sales order is not created within 6 months.
Acceptance Criteria
After completing the task, the backend service should be able to update the account stage.
Architecture Guidance
Definition
Active - An account has a sales order in the last 6 months
Inactive - An account doesn't have sales order in the last 6 months but had some so before.
Stage Update
Develop a scheduler job to change the account status to inactive if no Sales Order is generated within the last 6 months.
A scheduler job is run every night to verify the last Sales Order creation date. If it is more than 6 months, then the account stage shall be updated to inactive.
API Guidance

column4: Stop data sync events for transactions happen outside the New CRM
column6:
Overview
To prevent data sync loops between xCRM and the new CRM, data sync events will be generated only when entities are created or updated in the new CRM or mc1 application. Data sync events will be stopped in the following scenarios:
Data Migration: When data is migrated from xCRM to the new CRM (isMigrated flag = true).
Real-time Sync from xCRM: When entities are created or updated in xCRM and synced to the new CRM in real-time.

By implementing these rules, we can avoid unnecessary data sync loops and ensure seamless integration between the two applications. 
Current implementation
Currently only two entities have lookup field origin , that stores info about source service, that created this entity:
 account  - it uses lookup with xCRM and newCRM  values
 rfq  - it uses lookup with values CRM  and MC1  to decide direction of rfq processing. connect outbout events are produced only for MC1 
Scope
add origin  lookup field to ALL entities (to class BaseAuditFields )
add new common lookup with values: xCRM , CRM  and MC1 
* currently (since branch feature/CHP-1738-timeline-api ) we're setting createdBy  in BaseAuditFields  from userContext using common library code. The same code can be extended to set origin  using source of creation (`tokenSource`) from the user token:
MC1 - will be converted to lookup MC1 
CRM - will be converted to lookup CRM
API_KEY - will be converted to lookup xCRM  (TODO: review it in details - API_KEY is source for internal calls, they can be done both from connect and our service as well. We can use additional http header to distinguish types. If the header is not provided, source is considered as xCRM )
add logic to GenericEventPublisher (common lib) to the origin  field: if it is xCRM  - filter out the connect event, otherwise (including null value) - send event.
remove origin  from account model - no migration is required, BaseAuditFields.origin  will be compatible with it
remove origin  from rfq model - no migration is required, but additional check should be added rfqService  to consider xCRM  as inbound flow, CRM  - as outbound flow. In case of CRM  additional check should be done by category  field: if category is global  , value CRM  means new CRM (just CRM ), otherwise it is old CRM (`xCRM`)

column4: Remove mandatory attribute to support data migration
column6:
Summary
The xCRM records don't contain a few attributes that are marked mandatory in the new CRM app. To support the seamless data migration, the mandatory constraint should be removed. Here is a list entities 

Contact
1. The data model looks good for mandatory attributes
2. Need to review the codebase madatory attributes and decide to remove it
 
Account
1. The data model looks good for mandatory attributes
2. Need to review the codebase madatory attributes and decide to remove it
 
RFQ
1. The data model looks good for mandatory attributes
2. Need to review the codebase madatory attributes and decide to remove it
 
Quote
1. There are a few attributes made as optional (refer to updated_on column for 3rd June)
2. Need to review the codebase madatory attributes and decide to remove it
 
Sales Order
1. The data model looks good for mandatory attributes
2. Part is mandatory in each line, or it can be resolved from sourceDoc (RFQ, Quote) - Let's keep it as is.
 
Invoice
1. The data model looks good for mandatory attributes
2. In the codebase
    a. SO id is mandatory in the code. There are a lot of data without SO id in the xCRM. Currently, the migration script is filteing out SO by so.id != null. Let's review it further.
    b. Invoice line should have part -> no change in logic
 
User
1. The data model looks good for mandatory attributes
2. Need to review the codebase madatory attributes and decide to remove it
    a. department, emailConfirmed, etc.
3. In MongoDB collection - needs to be reviewd for unique index

column4: Compare Parts - Refactor Compare Alternatives to be later reused
column6:
There's a CompareAlternatives component which is used in Part entity → Details tab → Show alternatives → click checkboxes → Compare
Example: https://dev.chip1.info/parts/670940a028e8c43648567e64?globalPartId=25427704&partial=false 

Extract more common components (for now it can be in /apps/myChip1/src/features/CompareParts  folder). These common components (as building blocks) should be sufficient to recreate the CompareAlternatives component. Also the same components should allow to create a different view for comparing any list of parts which can be embedded into a modal (let me name it CompareParts here, feel free to use different name in code).

For testing purposes, you can create a modal and place CompareParts component in it, also provide hardcoded list of ids to that component/modal, so that it can fetch part details and show on UI.

Differences:
CompareParts will not have "Original" and "Alternative" sections
Instead of Original section, just show 3 columns (parts), which can be scrolled right/left with the right/left buttons on the screen
Add "Remove Part" button above the part image (as a separate row)
Make sure 1 or several sections are expanded in modal view as well (see current implementation on Part Details)

Reference design (which is not finalized, stick to the current look on MC1)

NEW DESCRIPTION
If mc1userId  is present:
Copy  functionality doesn't need to call backend, UI can generate the project url and share it.
In  Share  functionality, UI will have to call send-notification  api in mc1-core  to send an email.

column4: Change account stage from prospect, inactive to active via databits events
column6:
Summary
As a developer, I would like to develop a capability to change the account stage from in-active to active while a sales order is created.
Acceptance Criteria
After completing the task, the backend service should be able to update the account stage.
Architecture Guidance
Definition
Prospect - No sales order in life
Active - An account has a sales order in the last 6 months
Inactive - An account doesn't have sales order in the last 6 months but had some so before.
Stage Update
a. inactive to active 
When rfq/quote/SO is created in an account with the inactive stage, it should be updated to the active stage through the databits event.
b. prospect to active
When a SalesOrder is generated in an account with the prospect stage, it should be updated to the active stage through the databits event.
API Guidance

column4: Merging accounts & contacts inbound payload and handling it separately in the connect service
column6:
Summray
We are observing a payload processing order issue while the Connect service is processing accounts and contacts payloads. It is expected that the contact should be processed before the account payload is processed. However, due to the asynchronous nature of processing both payloads, the correct order is not maintained all the time.
To resolve the ordering issue, the xCRM would merge the account and contact payloads and publish it to the Connect service. The Connect service shall process two entities separately and make the downstream service call accordingly.

column4: Enhance fn-connect to share trackingId in migration publish response
column6:
Issue:
 There was a mismatch in the correlation IDs used during the processing of payloads published by the data team. Specifically, the correlation ID generated during the initial API call differed from the one used by the event processor, leading to inconsistencies in traceability and debugging.

Resolution:
 Introduced a custom logic in the Connect service to generate and propagate a unified correlation ID across both the API invocation and subsequent event processing stages. This ensures consistent tracking and improves observability across the entire workflow.

Deployment:
This change will be added as a hotfix and deployed to production post QE team signoff.

column6:
Overview
Recent testing of the project creation feature with large BOMs (approximately 1000 items) led to a surge in messages in two topics: mychip1-prod_chip1-transaction-async-processing-prod  (prod env) and mychip1-non-prod_chip1-transaction-async-processing-stage  (stage env). The async job processor triggered multiple events, including part lookup, auto RFQ, and sourcing plan, raising events to timeline resulting in Consumer Lag due to slow processing rates and Kafka broker rebalancing issues. To resolve this, we need to reassess the asyncJobProcessor's usage across multiple entities and optimize its performance.
Refer to Kafka consumer lag keeps increasing when users upload big BoM files (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2751)
Test Case
Multiple projects are created simultaneously with large BOMs (~ 1000 items) in the MC1 & CRM app.
Observations
Here is a list of observations during the debug time 

Large project timeline payload 
When creating projects with a large number of items, a substantial volume of timeline events is generated, resulting in large payloads. Currently, the request size limit is set to 32 MB. To optimize performance, we need to reduce the payload size. 
Immediate Mitigation  : 
Increased Max Payload size to 32 MB. spring.kafka.producer.properties.max.request.size  : 32 MB 
For addressing Large project timeline payload issue in above mentioned, as timeline for Project is not critical at the moment, as work around returned Project context as null, so that Project payload is not published.
Variety of exceptions during item processing
co.altir.chip1.transaction.models.dto.RfqDto.getMc1Users() is null
co.altir.chip1.transaction.models.dto.RfqDto.getOrigin() is null
Immediate Mitigation: Did proper error handling and pushed the code changes. 
Messages are piling up in specific partitions
While messages are pushed to the topic with the key (project ID), the specific partitions are getting full. It causes a bottleneck in message adding to that particular topic
Immediate Mitigation: Overridden max poll record to 100 spring.kafka.consumer.max-poll-records : 100 as observed that default number of polled msg(500) were not returning the response in the given timeout so messages were not being commit and contributing to msg consumer lag
Broker Message size was increasing and cluster went offline
Message size in broker increased drastically and adding additional 20GB also consumed immediately.  Cluster went down because of OOM. 
Immediate Mitigation  : 
Reduced message retention period to 10 days for most of the topics
Timeline has around 70GB storage size marked msg retention period to 0 days to delete the messages & later updated to 1 day.
Other Immediate Mitigations
Removed auto scaling and made 6 instance of transaction available so that consumer lag is reduced. Now added auto scaling back for transaction
Increased memory size to 4GB and monitoring max memory utilisation is 1.2 GB. Will be reduced back to 3 GB.

Potential Solution Identified
 Split the events by their type into their own topics to enable parallel processing - as far as I understand there is no direct dependencies between any of them/their types and we can just receive results of their processing as they are available independently. Create separate topics for part pricing, auto rfq, timeline event and as needed, so that after part details processing is done, corresponding messages can be pushed specific topic to avoid piling up all the message in one topic
Check if we really need all of the events generated per part and if so, if they can be batched - I remember I saw some individual part processing (100 parts = 100 extra events vs 100 parts = 5 events or so, depending on the total in BOM, for external look up) for auto-RFQs 
Review event handlers to see if the normal processing can be optimized (e.g., if we're doing expensive I/O for each event and some results can be cached/shared)
If nothing can be optimized in terms of events count and handler performance, another thing to try may be delayed publishing of those extra events, to help prioritize part look up (and whatever else may be time-sensitive from the user perspective) which is kind of dirty, but may help, at least from the perspective of the person who uploaded the BOM first (other concurrent users may wait longer)
Ask as part of this ticket
As part of this ticket lets address potential solution #1 mentioned in above potential solution lets focus on splitting async transaction topics and publish message to those specific topics. Also review next potential solutions #2 and #3 mentioned above. 
Also as to mitigate, Large project timeline payload  issue we have stopped publishing timeline events for Project so we can get them back. 
Need to use projects with needed payload, observed that full project details is pushed to timeline contributing for increase in payload size specially with projects with more number of line. 
Cross check are we publishing timeline event for every part line in the project or doing it only once per project to add project context? Is it needed to add for each part, if yes need to use projection to send payload.

column6:
Background
In the legacy MyChip1 app, the useRegionCurrency() hook was used to determine the account's default currency and display prices accordingly. In the new CRM, there's no default account — users navigate across multiple accounts and related entities. However, useRegionCurrency() was copied into CRM and now always returns USD, which is incorrect and misleading.
 Objective
Identify and eliminate incorrect usage of useRegionCurrency() in the CRM.
Replace it with the correct entity-specific currency (if available from backend).
Provide feedback to backend team for entities missing currency info.
Eventually remove useRegionCurrency() entirely from CRM.
 Feature Owner
QE Team (in collaboration with FE and BE)
 Acceptance Criteria
AC1: Audit Hook Usage
Locate and document all instances of useRegionCurrency() in the CRM codebase.
Categorize usage by entity type (Projects, Orders, Quotes, etc.).
AC2: Replace Hook With Entity Currency
For each usage:
Verify if the corresponding API response includes a currency field.
If present:
Update the frontend code to use entity.currency.
Validate that price displays (e.g., ₹, €, $, etc.) reflect the actual currency from entity.

Overview
This is an enhancement of Chip1 Inventory & IC Source Pricing Integration in MC1->Part Pricing (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2456)
A similar pricing model should be implemented in the Project→Pricing  tab.
Use Case 1 – Chip 1 Inventory + OEM Secrets (Primary Flow)
Check Chip 1 Inventory:
If available, display the inventory price along with other details.
On the mc1 and the new crm app, the same record shall be displayed.
Display OEM Secrets Data:
If Chip 1 Inventory returns no result, then continue to display the OEM secret price as it currently functions.
On the mc1 and the new crm app, the same record shall be displayed. The existing pricing logic for OEM Secrets should be intact.
Use Case 2 – IC Source (Fallback Flow)
If Chip 1 Inventory and OEM Secrets return no results, then display the pricing from IC Source.
On the MC1 app -
Similar to the part pricing approach, we want to display a single record with the Chip1 logo.
Clicking upon the line item, the price details side panel should not open.
On the new CRM app -
  shall confirm the price display.
Clicking upon the line item, the price details side panel should open and display the pricing from all IC Source vendors.

Approach
The backend service should fetch all project item prices from partprices collection through a single DB call.
The backend service shall process the response data based on the JWT token (MC1 or new CRM).
The response should have the price details based on the above criteria. The backend service should process it on the part pricing data set.

column4: Enhance service dependencies between mc1-core and account
column6:
Summary
Currently mc1-core uses direct db read/write calls to account-related entities (crm account and contact). This approach has several problems:
api-hub can be out-of-sync for the same entities in mc1-core and account service
we're using common lib with common custom mappers, that calculates some fields (origin, dataGroups). It can be the problem if mc1-core and account service will use the different common lib versions
we have additional logic that is triggered by account service on account update (redis cache refresh, timeline publishing, project events publishing), it is not executed by mc1-core now
Scope
Move all write (ideally, read as well) operations to internal endpoints and use them via account client

column4: Saving CRM Account IDs for MC1 App Entities
column6:
Overview
Issue with Current Implementation
Our backend services currently don't resolve the mc1AccountId for all entities created in the mc1 app. While this approach worked for mc1, which doesn't use CRM account IDs, it poses a challenge for our new CRM application.

Requirement for the new CRM Application
The new CRM app relies on CRM account IDs and references the same entities as mc1. To ensure seamless integration and data management, we need to:

Resolve the crmAccountId for

column4: New CRM ID generation for transactional entities
column6:
Overview
As a developer, I would like to generate a user-friendly ID for all transactional entities, i.e., RFQ, Quote, SalesOrder, Invoice.
This is an enhancement of New CRM ID generation and xCRM ID sync for Account, and Contact (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2464)
Architecture Guidance
Refer to the task New CRM ID generation and xCRM ID sync for Account, and Contact (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2464)
User-friendly ID pattern
Entity Header
The ID pattern shall be similar to the xCRM Number. DE25QU-XXXXXX 
- The first pair represents the region - US, DE, HK, CN 
- The second pair represents the current year - 25 
- The third pair represents the short name of an entity 
RFQ - RQ
Quote - QU
Sales Order - SO
Invoice - INV
- Hyphen(-) is part of the ID pattern
- XXXXX is a 6-digit sequence number for each entity.
Entity Item 
The entity line item should also have a unique, user-friendly ID. Pattern is header_id.sequence_number  DE25QU-XXXXXX.01
Request item ID - DE25RQ-XXXXXX.01
Quote item ID - DE25QU-XXXXXX.01A (explained below)
Sales Order item ID - DE25SO-XXXXXX.01
Requirement item ID pattern
As the xCRM doesn't support multiple items in an RFQ, multiple RFQs shall be created for a multi-part RFQ. Each item shall be an RFQ in the xCRM and have a reqNumber. So each RFQ item should save both IDs from the xCRM and the new CRM.
Quote item ID pattern
Quote item ID generation norm shall be different than other entities. As the quote item is linked to the RFQ item, the ID should have some correlation with the RFQ item ID. Here is the idea - 
Let's consider the RFQ ID DE25RQ-123456, which has two items.
The line items' IDs are DE25RQ-123456.01, DE25RQ-123456.02
Now, a quote DE25QU-987654 is created for the RFQ line DE25RQ-123456.01, which has three options.
The Quote Item ID pattern would be {quoteID}.{reqline_sequence}{alphabetic sequence} , i.e., DE25QU-987654.01A, DE25QU-987654.01B, DE25QU-987654.01C
If another quote DE25QU-765445 is created for the RFQ line DE25RQ-123456.02, which has two options. Then the quote items' IDs would be DE25QU-765445.02A, DE25QU-765445.02B
For a standalone quote
Since the standalone quote is not associated with any rfq items, the ID pattern always has {reqline_sequence} as 01 . Example - DE25QU-XXXXXX.01A, DE25QU-XXXXXX.01B

Downstream Service changes
Develop a user-friendly ID generation capability for RFQ, Quote, SalesOrder, and Invoice entities in the chip1-account service.
A new attribute crmNumber is introduced in all entities.
The user-friendly ID shall be saved in {entity}Number and crmNumber attribute.
The entity items should have two attributes - 
itemNumber - It will save the item ID as generated by the ID generator.
 sequenceNumber - It will store the sequence number, the later part after the dot ( . ) in the ID.
After the data sync with the xCRM
The existing xCRM generates the xCRM Number and CRM ID.
The connect should replace the user-friendly ID with the xCRM Number.
The connect should save the CRM ID in the entity as well.
As the data migration payload carries the xCRM Number, no need to generate a user-friendly ID in the new CRM.

Saving the xCRM Number and user-friendly IDs
As a new entity is created in the new CRM, the user-friendly ID shall be generated for the entity header and entity items.
A new attribute shall be introduced in all entities, and save the user-friendly ID.
After the data sync, the xCRM Number is generated and saved as is, like the current implementation.

column4: Enhance the entity filtering logic by dataGroup
column6:
Summary
Currently, the entity filtering is happening by accounts. The new CRM authorization mechanism determines the list of accounts based on the user's permission. Access to entities is derived based on the list of accounts. As the account is a rapidly growing entity in the system, this entity filter solution may not be scalable in the future.
The new approach is to consider the dataGroup to filter out entities.
The common entity filtration should bring the dataGroup as the core logic and discard the account.
The dataGroup[] should be introduced in all entities (project, rfq, Quote, SO, Invoice, Part Activity, tag, note)  that are associated with an account. 
The data migration should be considered to add the dataGroup[] in all entities.
The new records should be able to add dataGroup[]
Architecture

column4: Standardize Packaging Display Across Vendor Responses
column6:
Description:
 Right now when packaging is missing from OEM Secrets we show Packaging: –, but for Chip1 inventory and IC Source we still show NA or N/A. This looks odd when combining multiple vendor responses since NA shows up multiple times.
Acceptance Criteria:
If packaging is NA or empty from any source (Chip1, IC Source, OEMSec), display it as Packaging: –.
If at least one vendor has valid packaging data, display only that and omit NA/empty. Example: Tube, NA, empty → show Tube.
If the same packaging type comes from multiple vendors, show it only once. Example: Reel, Reel, Cut Tape → show Reel, Cut Tape.

Summary
 Enable downloading data from the Pricing tab of the Project entity as an Excel file, with two variations.
Variation 1:
 Export the data exactly as it appears on the screen (same fields and layout).
 → This download option should be available in MC1.
Variation 2:

Include the Franchise URL (as shown on the website for ordering) in both variations, if available.
Architecture
Let's develop an endpoint in the chip1-transaction service to download the pricing data as an Excel file.
The same endpoint can be used in both applications with an additional piece of information.
We need to ensure the mc1 app doesn't have access to all vendor pricing (Variation 2). It can be determined through the JWT token.

column4: Hierarchy management in the semiconductor parts
column6:
Overview
The new CRM has introduced two key upgrades:
1. Onboarding system components
2. Category-based browsing for a better user experience

However, existing semiconductor components lack clear category definitions, which hinders category-based browsing. To achieve a uniform user experience, we need to define a category hierarchy for semiconductors that aligns them with the new CRM's browsing capabilities.
Approach
Hierarchy Mapping sheet -   
Level 1 → Semiconductor
Level 2 & Level 3 shall be derived from the mapping sheet.

A new endpoint shall be developed in the chip1-parts service to map the category hierarchy for the existing parts.
The category hierarchy mapping should be derived automatically for the new parts. While the part details are fetched from the Silicon Expert platform, the category mapping should be resolved.

How category levels are calculated for parts
Each part 3 level categories (taxonomy path):
e.g. 
Linear > Amplifiers > Special Purpose Amplifiers

Implemented system components (hardware) have 3 level structure, e.g.:
Hardware > CPU > Desktop
 
To reuse the same category API we should follow the same structure.
 we should map  global part category  (e.g. Linear) to  L1 based on sheet Wiki to SE Mapping - it is Semiconductor based on the sheet. It will be L1 category
L2 category is Linear
L3 category is Special Purpose Amplifiers
The following tree will be stored:
Semiconductor -> Linear -> Special Purpose Amplifiers
 
4. Only L1 == Semiconductor parts are displayed in Semiconductors tab in By Category screen

column4: Associating tags with accounts during data migration
column6:
Overview
In the xCRM app, the accounts are linked with tags and which helps account filtering. As the account migration happens, tags should be linked with accounts in the new CRM app.
Steps -
The xCRM tags should be migrated to the new CRM. Data migration through Connect - Tag entity (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2187)
The tag migration shall be happening through the connect service, hence it will store the ID mapping.
The xCRM account migration payload carries the tags. The connect service translates it to the mc1 tags through the ID reconciliation process.
The downstream service should save the tags using the Tag Link API along with the account details.

column4: User id reconciliation in the Connect service
column6:
Overview
During user migration, the username attribute wasn't considered, resulting in missing username references in Connect and downstream services. Conversely, the username was passed during other entities' migration, preventing user entity reference creation. To resolve this, usernames will be replaced with user IDs, and Connect will resolve new CRM user references via ID reconciliation. This change is needed across all entities.

List of Entities with User attribute:
1. Account ==> lastEmp
             => Address ==> vatVerifiedBy
             => tags ==> addedBy
2. Contact ==> lastEmp
3. RFQ  ==> salesperson
             ==> lastEmp
4. Quote  ==> lastEmp
                 ==>  salesperson
5. SO  ==> lastEmp
           ==> salesperson
           ==> soAllowShippingBy
           ==> buyer
     ==> soLine ==> lastEmp
6. Invoice  ==> lastEmp
                  ==> salesperson
                  ==> shippedBy
                  ==> lastEmp

column9: Thanks!

column6:
Minimizing payload
We need to send only fields which are updated and ignore fields which are not touched (10 fields in a form, 2 updated -> UI sends only 2 fields).
Updates for RFQ (or Order) lines
Current logic is the next: take original rfq, find the rfq line in lines, replace it with the updated line (from a form) and send update request to the whole rfq. This is not the best approach in many ways.
We should send just one line in rfq.lines with only id and changed fields (with id on a root level)
{...,
 "id": "rfq_id",
 "items": [{"id":"rfq_item_id", "unset": ["description"]}]
}
Clearing fields
When an optional value is set in a form and saved, and I want to erase this value, we could send null but backend ignored this value and sent me back the previous value.
We should send unset  array with a list of fields which are cleared
List of objects where each has no id 
Send the whole array any time it changes
Diff notes
Lookup objects which have different shapes ( { key, value }  in the original version and full { namespace, category, key, value, ... }  in the patch version should be treated as the same if keys are the same
Empty string in a field is treated as an unset value if original object had non-empty string value. If that doesn't work for some case, then we can to provide a config option to control that
This approach should be used in all the places where PATCH request is used
Comparison is based on depth-first search. There will be a limit of how deep it can go to avoid circular references

column6:
Part API - add a query parameter projection={"context":"PART_INFO"}  into api/part/parts/full-details where we display info pane or summary card. Here we skip fetching additional fields which should result in faster responses.
The returned version will have the next fields skipped:
data.pricing 
in data.globalPart :
parametricData 
origin 
originLastUpdated 
packagingData 
environmentData - will be present
history 
alternateParts 
The new projection PartInfo reduces payload size and optimizes backend services load.
column9: PartInfo projection is used by default. If any component requires full  data, then a new request is made. If full data is in cache, then  PartInfo projection will not be requested.   Side effects: part info pane or part card in other entity hero can load faster especially on slower network speeds and slower devices.     Some benchmarks:   Average values     For the same 22 parts, comparison between default API call and a call with PART_INFO projection. It shows drastic reduction in payload size and about the same response time. There was no throttling added, but on the slower networks there will be also reduction in time to send this smaller response.

# Add Sentry build secrets to Docker build pipelines for CRM and myChip1

## Action items

- Store `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` as a CI secret
- Pass it to the Docker build for both CRM and myChip1 via `--secret id=sentry_auth_token,env=SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`

## Context

PR [altirllc/chip1-webui#3514]() added Sentry source map upload support to the build process. The token must be injected as a BuildKit secret — not a `--build-arg` — so it is never stored in image layers (see [Dockerfile diff]()):

```shell
# myChip1
docker build -f apps/myChip1/docker/Dockerfile -t chip1-webui \
  --secret id=sentry_auth_token,env=SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN \
  ...

# CRM
docker build -f apps/crm/docker/Dockerfile -t crm-webui \
  --secret id=sentry_auth_token,env=SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN \
  ...
```

Requires BuildKit (default in Docker 23.0+; set `DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1` for older versions). The param is optional — if omitted, source maps won't be uploaded but the build will still succeed.

Summary
Since both the mc1 and new CRM applications are built on the same backend platform, data sharing between them is a standard practice. As the mc1 app is customer-facing, there are instances where the Chip1 business requires controlled data sharing from the new CRM to the mc1 app. To address this need, we aim to develop a feature in the new CRM that empowers users to share data with the mc1 app on demand, providing flexibility and control over data exchange.
UI Acceptance Criteria
1) In the create project modal under Track BoM health, add - Show in MyChip1 (the checkbox shall be unselected). When the user selects the checkbox, the project should be made visible in Mychip1
 
2) In the Projects List view - when share project CTA is selected and the user selects the accounts/contacts information and either copies the link or sends an email, then the shared project should be made visible in MC1
3) When the project is already visible in MC1, then the option Hide in MyChip1 will be displayed under the 3 dots
 
4) Make the available actions under 3 dots in the list view also inside the project as FAB options    - This can be easily done too, I hope? If not, we will park this for later
 
Backend Acceptance Criteria
Upon completion of development, new CRM users will have full control over data sharing from the new CRM to the mc1 app, enabling them to manage data exchange efficiently.
Architecture Guidance
We need this capability in two entities: Projects & RFQs
Data model
The data model sheet is here
Introduce a new attribute isPrivate in the project & rfq entities in the data model sheet.

APIs
Let's enhance the create & update APIs to add the isPrivate attribute in the payload.
The request must set the value in the API, and it must be saved in the MongoDB.

Data filter
Let's enhance the filter criteria of the following APIs to consider isPrivate values. If the value is false, then the data should appear.
/rfq  → rfq list view
/rfq/{id}  → rfq details view
/projects  → project list view
/projects/{projectId}  → project details view

Flag toggle
In the mc1 app, projects and RFQs will be created with the isPrivate  flag set to false by default. This setting will be non-editable for mc1 users, ensuring that all entities created in the mc1 app are automatically shared with the new CRM.
In the new CRM app, users will have the ability to set the isPrivate flag while creating projects and RFQs. The default value for this flag will be set to true, allowing users to control data sharing as needed.

How to determine the request originator, mc1 app, or the new CRM?

Overview
The Company-Research-Assistant service utilizes LLM capabilities to collect company profiles for accounts created in the CRM. Since this is an asynchronous process, the collected data is initially stored in a dedicated microservice data storage. To integrate this data into the application, it needs to be migrated to the Chip1 MongoDB collection. The Connect service will facilitate this data migration asynchronously, ensuring a seamless transfer of data between the microservices.
Acceptance Criteria
Upon completion of development, company data will be seamlessly transferred to the Chip1 MongoDB collection via the Connect service.
Architecture Guidance
The connect service enhancement is needed for the following 
A new topic shall be created for the async processing of the data
A new publish endpoint should be developed to put the data in the new topic.
Once the company data is ready, the Company-Research-Assistant service shall pass the data(payload) to the Connect service through the new publish endpoint. It will be a protected request.
After the data is published, the Connect service shall consume the data from the new topic and start processing.
The incoming payload shall contain the account ID, for which the company details are fetched.
The incoming payload should be converted into the company payload through jolt transformation.
Then the Connect service calls the [POST] /company-details endpoint to transfer the data to the downstream service.
Once the company details are saved, the Connect service receives the company ID. Then the Connect service makes a call to [PATCH] /accounts endpoint to link the company ID to the accounts. 
Note: The account ID is present in the source payload.
In the Connect service flow, the ID reconciliation and saving in the entity_id_mapping table are not required.

column4: Accounts service and Company Agent handshaking
column6:
Overview
As a developer, I aim to establish a seamless integration between the Account Service and the Company Agent Service, enabling the Account Service to retrieve company details for an account through a handshaking mechanism.
Acceptance Criteria
After the completion of the development
The company details should be fetched for all existing accounts.
The newly created accounts must be able to fetch the company details.
Architecture Guidance
Newly created accounts
While a new account is created, the Accounts Service should call the Company Agent Service asynchronously to fetch the company details.
It will be a single account processed in the Company Agent service to fetch the details.

Existing Accounts
A scheduler will be implemented in the Accounts Service to periodically process existing accounts and retrieve their corresponding company details.
The Company Agent service has an endpoint to accept a list of accounts and process them asynchronously.
The scheduler task will check the list of accounts that don't have the company ID, and pass them to the Company Agent service.
We need to determine the optimal scheduler frequency and batch size that the Company Agent can process efficiently, ensuring maximum throughput and performance.
We'll develop a scheduler start/stop mechanism that disables the scheduler once all existing accounts have been processed, ensuring efficient resource utilization.

We are facing a challenge regarding account ID handling during the Company agent and connect service integration. At present, all accounts are processed with crmAccountID, but the batch process will have to deal with mongo accountID. In order to uniform ID processing in the connect service, here is a proposal 
The Company Agent service DB will introduce a new column, origin. It will save the app name that creates the account. Aditya Chopra
The existing records will have the origin value xCRM. Aditya Chopra
The Company Agent service triggers the event to the connect service that will have origin value. Aditya Chopra
The connect service will read the origin in the event payload and decide the ID reconciliation step Nikhil Kumar Singh
 a. If the origin is xCRM then fetching the account mongo id through ID reconciliation.
 b. if the origin is CRM then skip the ID reconciliation process as the payload has account mongo id.
The connect service shall call the PATCH /account/{id} endpoint to update the company id in the account record. Nikhil Kumar Singh
A schedular task shall be developed in the accounts service to fetch the company data for the existing accounts. Nikhil Kumar Singh
 a. The task will pass the crmAccountID if the origin is xCRM in the payload to The Company Agent service
 b. The task will pass the account mongo id if the origin is CRM in the payload to The Company Agent service

A rule shall be configured in the events service to enable the delayed action feature for the chip1 tenant. The given doc has the guidelines for the same. The rule shall be 
The required configurations

Configured the delayed action rule
As the trigger time meets, the payload shall be routed to the notification topic - 
mychip1-non-prod__notifications-output for the non-prod env

The Events service will transform the source payload into a notification-compatible format, enabling further processing to commence.

column4: Create a single RFQ for Projects
column6:
Overview
Combine RFQs for Projects to a single RFQ with multiple lines
UI needs to send a patch call to the backend when a new RFQ is created from a project. The backend shall decide to create a new RFQ or add a new item in the existing RFQ based on the following scenarios - 
1) No RFQ exists create a new
2) RFQ exists but closed create a new
3) Multiple RFQ exisits (old project), create a new
4) In all other cases, use the existing RFQ

Overview
As a developer, I would like to develop a capability in the Connect service that helps Invoice data sync with xCRM.
Acceptance Criteria
After completion of the task, the connect service should be able to sync the Purchase Order entity to the xCRM platform.
Architecture Guidance
xCRM payload metadata details
Payload - inbound & outbound payload
Data sync endpoint - v1/obj/sendpotoxcrm 
Outbound flow
A Purchase Order is created/updated in the new CRM app. This record has to be synced to the xCRM. A data sync event shall be triggered from the downstream service to the Connect service.
As the PO is created in the xCrM, it should also create a Stock Line.
The outbound PO data sync payload shall have the Stock Line ID.
The xCRM response should have the Stock Line ID, and the connect service shall keep the Stock Line ID mapping.
The xCRM response contains crmId & poNumber . This must be written back to the downstream service as an PO update.
crmId → crmId 
poNumber → poNumber 
Inbound flow
An PO is created/updated in the xCRM app; it has to be synced to the CRM
As the PO is created in the system, it should create a Stock Line.
The PO update has no impact on the Stock Line.
The xCRM payload shall have the Stock Line ID.
As the Stock Line is created in the downstream service, the connect service shall keep Stock Line ID mapping.
The unmapped xcrm attributes should be mapped to the additionalAttribute and save it in the downstream system.
Data sync Flow diagram - here
 
To Be Completed
The xCRM payload shall contain the Stock Line ID. (Zohaib)
The xCRM response shall contain the Stock Line ID. (Zohaib)
The inbound PO data sync payload shall have the Stock Line ID. ( )
To Be Decided
How does the downstream service pass the Stock Line ID in the PO creation response? (  )
API Guidance

column4: Add Configurable Filter to Exclude Closed Items in List APIs for Transactional Entities
column6:
Task Description
Introduce a generic and consistent mechanism across all transactional entity List APIs to optionally exclude items that are in a closed state.
Objective
By default, List APIs should return only open (active) records.
Clients should be able to explicitly request closed items or all items using a simple request attribute (query param or filter flag).
This avoids repetitive client-side filtering and ensures consistent behavior across entities.
 Proposed API Behavior
Add a common request attribute, for example:

isClosed = false   (default)
Default behavior: Return only open records
Fetch only closed records if required
 Entities & Closed Status Definitions
RFQ
Closed statuses:
CLOSED
SO_CREATED
Default list behavior:
Exclude RFQs in CLOSED or SO_CREATED status
 Quote
Closed statuses:
FULLY_CLOSED
SUCCESSFULLY_CLOSED
UNSUCCESSFULLY_CLOSED
Default list behavior:
Exclude fully or partially closed quotes
 Sales Order
Closed statuses:
CLOSED
CANCELLED
Default list behavior:
Exclude closed or cancelled sales orders
 Invoice
Closed statuses:
CLOSED
COMPLETED
Default list behavior:
Exclude closed and completed invoices
 Acceptance Criteria
A single, consistent attribute is supported across all transactional List APIs.
Default behavior returns only open records.
Closed records are included only when explicitly requested.
No breaking change for existing consumers relying on default behavior.

column4: QE:: [CRM] Automate Account Timeline - All Events
Account & Contact sync to the xCRM
column6:
Overview
As a developer, I would like to develop a capability in the Connect service that helps to sync data for Accounts & Contacts when both entities are created together. A contact doesn't exist without an account. Hence, an account should be created first and then the contact shall be created with the context of the account.
Approach
The following is a proposed approach to solve the use case.
Backend service 
 1. An account is created with a new contact
   a. The account service would receive a consolidated payload with account & contact info from UI.
   b. The BE service must create an account first. (no data sync event trigger to connect now)
   c. Then, a contact will be created with the account details in it. (no data sync event trigger to connect now)
   d. After this, the account shall be updated with contact info
   e. The data sync event shall be triggered with account & contact info
 
2. An account is created with an existing contact
   a. The account service would receive a consolidated payload with account & contact with ID info from UI.
   b. The BE service must create an account first with contact ID
   c. Contact entity will be updated with account ID
   d. data sync event shall be triggered with account & contact details
 
3. The account is created without a contact
   a. The account service would receive a consolidated payload with the account from UI.
   b. The BE service must create an account. (Account data sync event trigger to connect)

Connect service 
 1. data sync event for new account & new/existing contact
   a. split the payload into two entities (account & contact)
   b. create an account first & save the crm account ID to the account collection
   c. Create a contact with the account ID. And save the crm contact ID in the contact collection
 
2. data sync event with account only
   a. Create an account first & save the crm account ID to the account collection

Offer Matching (  )
Offer matching Events
Let's build the Offer Matching feature as an event-driven capability. The following events should be raised for this purpose - 
Offers create/update
RFQ create/update
Sales Order create/update
Matching Criteria
The matching should occur based on MPN and manufacturer (Part ID).
When an offer is created
a) The MPN + Manufacturer is matched with any open RFQ (RFQ Status ≠ Closed).
b) The MPN + Manufacturer is matched with any open SO (SO status ≠ Procured or beyond).
When an RFQ or SO is changed (created/updated)
a) The MPN + Manufacturer is matched with any offer
b) RFQ Status ≠ Closed
c) SO status ≠ Procured or beyond

A notification will be triggered after the match occurs. (need more details)

column4: Rfq lines and Order lines list performance issue
column6:
Current implementation
Rfq lines and Order lines views use Mongo $unwind operation to retreive line level data.
The problems
Mongo filtering and sorting depends on existing indexes and the indexed search is not applied in a lot of cases (especially, for "globalSearch" - it is just several regexp queries applied in mongo)
Total count is not calculated correctly (now we usually count documents instead of count lines) - it is applicable for line views, dashboards, part sourcing tabs
Solution
We should use ES for filtering and sorting in the same way how it is used for document list views. The problem is, the additional ES indexes are required, that should contain one line and rest of other fields of document
This should be done as part of fn-db-management to support management of such additional indexes.

Overview
This task is an enhancement of Purchase Order management (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-3509). In the scope of this task, the stock line (receiving line) and allocation shall be handled.
Acceptance Criteria
After completion of the task, the backend should be able to perform
The stock line should be managed.
Allocation shall be handled.
Architecture Guidance
Functional use cases - here\
Functional Tasks - 
Datamodel -   
Figma design - here
Handshaking between Sales & Purchase entities
An RFQ is created.
A Sourcing Request is created from an RFQ.
The SR should have the RFQ ID, RFQ Item ID
The RFQ Item should also have the SR ID
RFQ Line and SR are maintaining the many-to-many relationship
Offers are created from a Sourcing Request
The Offer should have SR ID, RFQ ID, and RFQ Line ID
The RFQ Item should have the List of Offer IDs
A Quote is created from an Offer
The quote item must have the Offer ID, Sourcing Request ID, RFQ ID, RFQ Line ID
A Quote can be created from an RFQ
The quote item must have the RFQ ID and the RFQ Line ID
The quote item shall also have the Sourcing Request ID, Offer ID (if available)
A Sales Order is created from a Quote
The sales order should have RFQ ID, RFQ Line ID, Quote ID, Quote Item ID, Sourcing Request ID, Offer ID
The SR should have SO ID, SO Item ID
A Purchase Order is created from an Offer
The purchase order line should have Sourcing Request ID, Offer ID, SO ID, SO Line ID
The Sales Order line must have PO ID, PO Item ID
The Sourcing Request should have PO ID, PO Item ID
Purchase Order life cycle
 PO can be created from one/multiple offers, hence it will have one/multiple lines. The PO status and Item status are shipped. figma
When the item arrives in the warehouse, and the quantity does not match the PO Item quantity. Then the warehouse guy
 Updates the PO Item quantity
 The stock item shall be split. The stock item status would be Arrived. 
Status update figma, Stock Item figma
After this, the QC guy starts the receiving process. The receiving items shall be checked and updated in the system. For example, items have different Date Codes, Lot Codes; the item split shall happen further. The stock item status would be QC Ready. 
Start receiving figma, Stock Item figma
The QC process gets kicked off on the Stock Item. 
A PO is created
PO can be created from one/multiple offers
A PO shall be created with one or multiple lines. The status would be shipped.
ID management
IDs to be copied in the PO Line - SR ID, Offer ID, SOID, SO Item ID
The PO ID, PO Item ID should be copied to the Sales Order Item.
The Sourcing Request should have PO ID, PO Item ID
Records shall be created in the following -
pruchaseOrder
purchaseOrder.line
purchaseOrder.allocation
salesOrder.allocation
stock
A PO Item is updated
When the items arrive in the warehouse, the guy shall check and update the PO Item quantity if it is mismatched. In such case, the following happens -
The PO Item quantity must be updated.
The po.item.allocation and so.item.allocation quantity must be updated accordingly.
The Stock Item qty shall also be updated.
Allocation
The allocation is a process that defines how the PO items are mapped to the SO items. It is helping the shipping process.
When a Purchase Order is created, the system will automatically generate an allocation. 
It will make an entry in the po.line.allocation fragment, and salesOrder.line.allocation fragment.
If the stock line is split, the allocation has to be adjusted in the PO and SO.
If the PO qty and SO qty match, then no adjustment is required.
The allocation is linked to multiple SOs, then a notification shall be triggered to the sales manager for manual intervention. 
Stock
When a PO is created, an entry shall be created in the stock with the status Shipped.
Refer to Stock management (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-3672) for Stock data management.
Other enhancement
Upon QC completed for a stock item, mark the quantity of allocation in the SO as received.
Sales Order would now be marked as “Ready to Ship”

column6:
For the `/transaction/purchase/source-request` we have implemented a number of hacks on UI side to address missing API functionality. That must be addressed as soon as we have enough time.

Set default status during sourcing request creation. If the status doesn't come in the payload - set it to "Open"
Implement "POST /:id/merge" endpoint. Which will add an RFQ line to the sourcing request and merge their requirements. Currently, the required changes are as follow:
Sum up the quantity - add RFQ line's quantity to that of the sourcing request
Add RFQ info to the `typeDetails`
Payload will consist of `sourcingRequestId`, and RFQ line's info (whatever BE requires)
Implement "GET /:id/offers-summary" endpoint OR include the info in the sourcing request details response. The summary should contain:
Information about linked offers in each status, e.g. `{ "requested": 4, "received": 2 }`
Recommended offer. Currently, it's not clear what that is. It may be an offer in a certain status OR an offer having corresponding flag set to `true`
To maintain clean and robust flow, task must be automatically created on the backend side. Without additional request from UI.

column6:
Summary
Functional task - Create Quote from RFQ (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2563)
As a developer, I would like to enhance the existing Quote APIs to enable users to create and update requirements.
Acceptance Criteria
After completing the development
A quote can be created from an existing quote (duplicate).
A quote can be shared to the customer.
Architecture Guidance
Duplicate Quote - The system allows users to duplicate existing quote lines. Upon selecting this option, a new Adding New Item  modal will appear, pre-populated with data from the original line. When the user saves the new record, the system will trigger a [POST] /quote/{id}/item  API call to create the new quote line.
Send Quote
Develop an endpoint to send an email with a quote to the customer.
In this process, a PDF file shall be generated and saved in the S3. An email shall be triggered, with the PDF file attached.
MC1 Private Option
We need to develop a feature that allows new CRM users to control quote sharing to the MC1 app, enabling them to share quotes with customers via the MC1 app when they are ready.
Refer to this task for the implementation approach MC1 private option (https://app.clickup.com/t/8651091/CHP-2581)
Until the UI feature is rolled out in the production environment, let's set the isPrivate value is false, and the properties file will drive it.
